values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 comment)

Good catch.

Sadly, this gave the appearance of working when "lctl lustre_build_version" returned the expected result, but in fact that was always falling back to the compiled-in LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING.  I normally use "lctl get_param version".

parents.subject: LU-14125 osc: prevent overflow of o_dropped

commit.subject: LU-13153 lib: fix llapi_get_version_string
commit.message:
LU-13153 lib: fix llapi_get_version_string

llapi_get_version_string() has always been broken as it passes args to
get_lustre_param_value in the wrong order.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: 0c5fbd80f1ba ("LU-5969 lustreapi: replace llapi_get_version()")
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Ib4bdd715295f97fe5b7080f42bfa06883f191234
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/40680
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
